model.label: Claude Sonnet 4.6
model.description: Anthropic • Fast flagship Claude model for coding, analysis, and high-throughput agent use.

pick.rationale: Kansas enters as a 13-point favorite and holds a commanding edge in every measurable dimension: KenPom #21 and Torvik #22 with an AdjEM of +24.48, a top-10 nationally ranked defense (AdjDE 94.0), and a 23-10 record forged against elite Big 12 competition. The Jayhawks' most critical health concern was resolved with both 7'2" center Hunter Dickinson and senior guard Kevin McCullar Jr. expected to return for the tournament after injury absences, giving Kansas a near-full-strength roster that presents a severe size and athleticism mismatch for a WAC-tested Cal Baptist squad. California Baptist's 25-8 record and dramatic WAC Tournament title run are admirable, but the Lancers' lack of KenPom/Torvik ratings reflects a schedule that did not prepare them for a Big 12-caliber opponent, and their path to an upset — requiring them to neutralize Dickinson in the post and sustain offensive efficiency against a top-10 defense — is among the least credible 13-seed scenarios in the bracket.

reasoningStep.title: Kansas vs. California Baptist — Round of 64 Analysis
reasoningStep.summary: Kansas is a clear, well-supported favorite backed by elite efficiency metrics, a returning healthy roster featuring Hunter Dickinson, and a 13-point betting line. California Baptist is a credible WAC champion but lacks the profile to threaten a top-25 national team.
evidence: Kansas: KenPom #21, Torvik #22, AdjEM +24.48, AdjO 118.4 (rank 57), AdjD 94.0 (top-10 nationally). California Baptist has no KenPom or Torvik rating, consistent with a mid-major WAC program.
Kansas health score 90.7 per SIC Score injury report. Hunter Dickinson and Kevin McCullar Jr. both expected to return for the NCAA Tournament after missing the Big 12 Tournament with injuries.
Kansas opened as a 13-point favorite over Cal Baptist with a total of 135.5, per Action Network. The market strongly reflects the talent gap between a Big 12 #4 seed and a WAC champion.
California Baptist finished 25-8 (13-5 WAC) and won the WAC Tournament title with a dramatic 63-61 comeback over Utah Valley. Rick Croy is in his 13th year as head coach.
4-vs-13 upsets occur at roughly a 20% historical rate, but Cal Baptist's WAC schedule and absence from efficiency rating systems make them a below-average upset threat relative to typical 13-seeds.
